page.title: Guides for scoping, takeover, and software buying decisions | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Guides for companies clarifying a project before delivery starts
page.description: Guides for scoping a custom web app, taking over an existing app safely, pricing takeover work, SaaS vs internal tooling, AI fit in business processes, and deciding on senior contract support.
page.primaryQuery: Guides for companies clarifying a project before delivery starts

hero.title: Guides for pre-project clarity, not generic developer-blog traffic
hero.subtitle: For buyers trying to clarify scope, takeover, build-vs-buy, AI fit, or the need for senior contract capacity before delivery starts.

intro: These guide pages are designed to improve the quality of early project decisions and reduce avoidable delivery confusion.

The section covers project scoping, inherited-app takeover, takeover pricing, internal tool vs SaaS, and the question of when AI fits a business process versus when simpler automation or workflow changes should come first.

sections.title: What this section covers
body: Each guide is organised around a direct answer, a recommended approach, common mistakes, and the kind of decision result the guide should create.

sections.title: When this section is most useful
body: This section helps a buyer make a better decision before the project gathers momentum in the wrong direction.
bullets: buyers preparing the first serious delivery phase
teams clarifying scope or takeover decisions
companies pressure-testing build-vs-buy or staffing choices

sections.title: How to continue
body: Start with the child page closest to the current decision and then continue to the relevant service, case study, or inquiry path.
bullets: open the guide that matches the current uncertainty
continue into the linked service or template pages
move to inquiry once the decision is concrete enough to discuss directly

faq.question: Are these guides aimed only at technical teams?
faq.answer: No. They are written for commercial and delivery-side readers as much as for engineers.

faq.question: Do they connect back to the services?
faq.answer: Yes. Every guide is linked to the most relevant commercial path.

faq.question: Are they useful in urgent situations?
faq.answer: Yes, especially for inherited-app takeover and takeover pricing decisions.

for: buyers preparing the first serious delivery phase
teams clarifying scope or takeover decisions
companies pressure-testing build-vs-buy or staffing choices
notFor: passive reading with no live project context

cta.label: Discuss your project
cta.note: If this section matches a live project decision, a short summary is enough to continue.
